source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Crane, Other Lifting

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Mississippi Canyon Block 778

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Oil Discharge to Sea
actions_taken: Production operators pulled samples of overboard water for analysis
Oil and grease concentration analyses performed
Corrective action: additional training of personnel regarding proper communication of pollution events
event.summary: On 13 July 2020 at 1055 hours, a pollution event occurred at BP's Thunder Horse platform (MC 778-A) in the Gulf of Mexico. While ramping up production following pigging and flowback operations, production operators observed a sheen covering 90% of a 100-yard area. BP's failure to adequately flush workover fluids from subsea well MC 822 TD003 S45 before placing it into production resulted in abnormal emulsion and foaming in process vessels. This caused an estimated 3 barrels of oil to be discharged into the Gulf of Mexico through the overboard produced water handling system. The incident was not reported to the National Response Center or BSEE until 23 July 2020, 10 days after occurrence, violating reporting requirements.
recommendations: NOD office recommends OII coordinate with BSEE Office of Environmental Compliance/Environmental Monitoring Unit and Oil Spill Preparedness Division to determine agency response to ongoing sheen events and oil and grease limit exceedances for Thunder Horse facility
key_phrases: human performance error - inattention to task

threats.name: Inadequate Workover Fluid Cleanup Before Well Startup
Mixture of Process and Workover Fluids in Production System
Abnormal Foaming in Crude Oil Treater and Separators
Oil Overflow from Vessel Weir to Water Dump Line
Failure to Report Pollution Event to Regulatory Authorities

consequences.name: Oil Discharge to Gulf of Mexico Waters

controls.name: Workover Fluid Cleanup Verification Procedure
controls.barrier_role: Prevent mixing of workover and process fluids
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to prevent hazardous fluid mixture

supporting_text: BP decided to place the well into production before verifying that the workover fluids were cleaned up
BP placed the MC 822 TD003 S45 subsea well into production before verifying that the workover fluids were cleaned up

controls.name: Produced Water Quality Monitoring and Sampling
controls.barrier_role: Detect oil contamination in produced water discharge
controls.lod_basis: Detection of abnormal water quality conditions

supporting_text: BP production operators also documented the Crude Oil Treater #1 having dirty water
BP's production operators pulled samples of the overboard water to send in for analysis
the production operators observed a sheen which covered 90% of a 100-yard area

controls.name: Pollution Event Reporting to Regulatory Authorities
controls.barrier_role: Ensure timely notification of pollution events to NRC and BSEE

supporting_text: The sheen was reported to BP's management but was not reported to the National Response Center (NRC) or Bureau of Safety and Environmental Enforcement (BSEE) on 13 July 2020
On 23 July 2020, BSEE New Orleans District (NOD) office was notified by BP of a pollution event that occurred on 13 July 2020
BSEE found that BP did not notify the BSEE NOD office per the requirements of their EPA permit

controls.name: Vessel Foaming and Emulsion Control
controls.barrier_role: Prevent abnormal foaming and emulsion in process vessels
controls.lod_basis: Prevent overflow of oil from vessel weir to water dump line

supporting_text: The mixture of process and workover fluids may have caused abnormal emulsion and foaming in several vessels such as the Crude Oil Treater and Intermediate Pressure separator
The emulsion and foaming may have crossed the vessel's oil weir into the water dump line

controls.name: Production Ramp-Up Procedure and Verification
controls.barrier_role: Ensure system readiness before increasing production rates
controls.lod_basis: Verify all systems are clean and operational before production increase

supporting_text: Once the flowback and pigging operations were completed, the production operators started ramping up production
While ramping up to final production target rates on 13 July 2020, at approximately 1055 hours, the production operators observed a sheen

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
